architecture.summary: StoC-ToT is a prompting framework for multi-hop question answering that constructs a tree of reasoning paths by prompting an LLM to generate sub-questions at each reasoning level, answer them using constrained decoding from a vocabulary bank built from evidence, estimate validity probabilities for each path across question/reasoning/answer levels, and aggregate path probabilities to select the best answer. For closed-source LLMs, constraints are enforced via prompting; for open-source LLMs, a logit processor sets non-vocabulary tokens to negative infinity at decode time.

biblio.title: STOC-TOT: Stochastic Tree-of-Thought with Constrained Decoding for Complex Reasoning in Multi-Hop Question Answering

claims.statement: StoC-ToT with GPT-4 improves Exact Match accuracy by 7% and F1 score by 7.8 points over original tree-of-thought prompting on the HotpotQA dataset

On HotpotQA, StoC-ToT attains an on-average increase in performance of over 6% compared with vanilla prompting on GPT models, and the improvement goes up to 11% when constrained decoding is implemented
On the MuSiQue dataset with GPT-4, StoC-ToT shows an 11.5% EM improvement (from 31.50 to 42.0) over zero-shot vanilla prompting
StoC-ToT surpasses the original Tree-of-Thought prompting by 7% with the GPT4 model on both tested datasets

Constrained decoding, which makes the model much more grounded to evidence when answering the question, effectively addresses hallucination issues and improves the overall performance of the framework

StoC-ToT performs better at both Bridge Questions and Sequential Questions, suggesting that it can avoid reasoning dead-ends and is better at forming intermediate reasoning lines than Chain-of-Thought and Tree-of-Thought

StoC-ToT performs better on both reasoning types, especially on questions containing parallel reasoning, suggesting that its stochastic way of forming the tree is very effective when solving questions containing multiple reasoning paths
The framework relies on initiating multiple model instances and requires multiple prompts per round, imposing heavy time costs even after implementing the paraphrase module
